2606.06223.title: From Reward-Hack Activations to Agentic Risk States: Context-Calibrated Mechanistic Monitoring in LLM Agents
2606.06223.paper_summary: Language-model agents act through repeated cycles of observation, reasoning, and action selection, making safety monitoring depend on both internal model state and environment context. We study reward-hacking monitors in ReAct-style agents acting in Gameable ALFWorld and WebShop. Agents are instrumented with activation-based reward-hack scores, token-level entropy, and decision-context features. We find that adapters fine-tuned on \textit{School-of-Reward-Hacks} dataset can transfer reward-hack tendencies into agentic action selection, especially when the environment exposes proxy-reward affordances. However, mitigating such behavior cannot rely on activation dynamics alone. High reward-hack activation identifies a latent policy state, but does not necessarily imply an immediate exploit action. Across next-step prediction tasks, entropy and context-calibrated internal features improve risk estimation over reward-hack activation alone. Activation-direction steering further reduces proxy-exploit behavior in selected mixed-adapter regimes. Overall, our results support context-calibrated internal monitoring for agents: reward-hack activation identifies a latent policy state, while entropy and decision context help determine when that state becomes risky action.
2606.06223.paper_summary_zh: 語言模型代理透過重複的觀察、推理和行動選擇循環進行操作，使得安全監控依賴於內部模型狀態和環境上下文。我們研究在可遊玩ALFWorld和WebShop中行動的ReAct風格代理的獎勵駭客監控器。代理被裝置以基於激活的獎勵駭客得分、令牌級熵和決策上下文特徵。我們發現，經過\textit{School-of-Reward-Hacks}數據集微調的適配器可以將獎勵駭客傾向轉移到代理行動選擇中，特別是當環境暴露出代理獎勵的便利性時。然而，緩解此類行為不能僅依賴於激活動態。高獎勵駭客激活識別出潛在的政策狀態，但不一定意味著立即的利用行動。在下一步預測任務中，熵和上下文校準的內部特徵改善了對獎勵駭客激活的風險估計。激活方向引導進一步減少了在選定的混合適配器模式中的代理利用行為。總體而言，我們的結果支持對代理的上下文校準內部監控：獎勵駭客激活識別出潛在的政策狀態，而熵和決策上下文有助於確定該狀態何時變成風險行動。
2606.06223.author: Patrick Wilhelm et.al.
